title: Vegetable Biryani

overview.description: Fragrant basmati rice layered with spiced vegetables and gently steamed together using the traditional dum method.

method: Wash the basmati rice until the water runs clear.
Soak for 30 minutes, then drain.
Bring a large pan of salted water to the boil.
Add the bay leaves, cardamom, cinnamon stick and cloves.
Cook the rice until about 70% cooked, then drain immediately.
Heat the oil or ghee over a medium heat.
Cook the onion until golden.
Add the garlic, ginger and green chillies and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin seeds, ground coriander, ground cumin, turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder.
Cook for 30 seconds.
Add the potatoes, carrots and cauliflower.
Cook for 8–10 minutes.
Stir in the yoghurt and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the peas, garam masala, coriander, mint and lemon juice.
Season to taste.
Spread half the rice over the vegetables.
Cover with the remaining rice.
Scatter over the crispy fried onions.
Cover tightly with a lid.
Cook over a very low heat for 20 minutes.
Rest for 10 minutes before gently fluffing with a fork.

learning.did-you-know: The word 'biryani' comes from a Persian word meaning 'fried before cooking', reflecting its Persian influence and layered cooking method.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to parboil basmati rice, prepare a vegetable masala and finish a biryani using the traditional dum steaming technique.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A complete vegetarian meal that showcases authentic biryani technique while using everyday vegetables from the pantry and freezer.
chapman-tips: Don't fully cook the rice before layering.
Keep the heat very low during the final steaming stage.
Fresh mint makes a noticeable difference to the finished dish.
Let the biryani rest before fluffing to prevent the rice breaking.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions. Reheat gently with a splash of water.
notes.leftovers: Excellent reheated the following day or served with a fried egg.
